c.d: The range of operating systems and devices on which the app is available (e.g., Windows, macOS, iOS, Android, Web).

c.d: The variety and depth of functionalities offered by the app, such as text formatting, multimedia support, collaboration tools, and integrations.

c.d: The design, ease of use, and overall user experience provided by the app.
c.s: User reviews, professional app reviews, usability studies

c.d: The app's ability to sync notes across devices and its support for cloud storage services.
The measures taken by the app to ensure user data security and privacy, including encryption and compliance with privacy standards.
c.s: Official app websites, privacy policies, security whitepapers

c.d: The cost structure of the app, including free tiers, subscription plans, and one-time purchase options.
c.s: Official app websites, app store listings

c.d: The app's capability to function without an internet connection.
Tools and functionalities that allow multiple users to work together on notes or projects within the app.
c.s: Official app websites, feature comparison articles

c.d: The ability to customize the app's appearance and functionality, including support for plugins, themes, and integrations.
c.s: Official app websites, user manuals, developer documentation

c.d: The app's responsiveness, stability, and reliability during use.
